title: helianthemum sauvagei
intro: Helianthemum sauvagei, also known as rockrose or wild rosemay, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its pink to purple flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, Helianthemum sauvagei has been used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
